3.happening: About to copy list1 into l1_rem — a working array we'll shrink as we consume nodes.
3.why: Copying lets us pop from the front to track what remains visible in the list1 chain.
3.invariant: list1 and list2 are unmodified; l1_rem is about to be initialized.

4.happening: About to copy list2 into l2_rem.
4.why: Same pattern: a shrinking array tracks list2's remaining nodes.
4.invariant: l1_rem is ready; l2_rem is about to be initialized.

5.happening: About to create result = [] — the growing merged output.
5.why: result will accumulate nodes in sorted order as we splice them in.
5.invariant: l1_rem and l2_rem are ready; result is about to be created.

6.happening: About to set splice_source = None — will record which list each node comes from.
6.why: The visualizer uses splice_source to animate the node flying from list1 or list2 into result.

7.happening: About to set splice_val = None — will hold the value just spliced for the animation label.
7.why: Storing splice_val separately lets the visualizer label the flying node correctly.

8.happening: Both lists still have nodes — entering the comparison loop.
8.why: As long as both lists have remaining nodes, we must choose the smaller front to splice next.
8.invariant: result is sorted so far. l1_rem has {len(l1_rem)} nodes, l2_rem has {len(l2_rem)} nodes.

8.happening: At least one list is exhausted — exiting the main loop.
8.why: Once either list is empty there is nothing left to compare; we drain the other.
8.invariant: result has {len(result)} nodes in sorted order.

9.happening: l1_rem[0] = {l1_rem[0]} ≤ l2_rem[0] = {l2_rem[0]} — list1's front wins (or ties).
9.why: The smaller (or equal) front is always safe to append — it can't be out-of-order with anything remaining.
9.invariant: result is sorted; the next node to splice comes from list1.

9.happening: l1_rem[0] = {l1_rem[0]} > l2_rem[0] = {l2_rem[0]} — list2's front is smaller.
9.why: The else branch fires when list2 wins the comparison.
9.invariant: result is sorted; the next node to splice comes from list2.

10.happening: About to record splice_source = 'list1' — the animation will fly a node from the list1 chain.
10.why: Labelling the source before the splice keeps visualizer state one step ahead.
10.invariant: l1_rem[0] = {l1_rem[0]} is about to be spliced.

11.happening: About to peek splice_val = l1_rem[0] = {l1_rem[0]} for the animation label.
11.why: Storing the value before pop ensures the label survives the structural change.
11.invariant: splice_source = 'list1'; splice_val is about to be set to {l1_rem[0]}.

12.happening: About to splice {splice_val} from list1 — popping it from l1_rem and appending to result.
12.why: This is the core merge step: the smaller node leaves its source chain and joins the result.
12.invariant: result will have {len(result)} + 1 nodes; l1_rem will shrink by one.

14.happening: About to record splice_source = 'list2' — the animation will fly a node from the list2 chain.
14.why: list2's front was smaller; it becomes the next node in the result.
14.invariant: l2_rem[0] = {l2_rem[0]} is about to be spliced.

15.why: Same pattern: preserve the value before the pop so the label is available for the animation.
15.invariant: splice_source = 'list2'; splice_val is about to be set to {l2_rem[0]}.

16.happening: About to splice {splice_val} from list2 — popping it from l2_rem and appending to result.
16.why: list2's front is smaller; splicing it maintains sorted order in result.
16.invariant: result will have {len(result)} + 1 nodes; l2_rem will shrink by one.

17.happening: List2 is exhausted — list1 still has {len(l1_rem)} remaining nodes to drain.
17.why: Since list2 is empty, all remaining list1 nodes are already in sorted order relative to result.
17.invariant: result has {len(result)} nodes; l1_rem = {l1_rem} will be appended one-by-one.

17.happening: List1 is also empty — nothing left to drain from list1.
17.why: Both lists were consumed in the main loop; fall through to check list2.
17.invariant: result has {len(result)} nodes in sorted order.

18.happening: About to record splice_source = 'list1' for the drain animation.
18.why: Each drain step animates the same way as a regular splice — the node flies from list1 to result.
18.invariant: l1_rem[0] = {l1_rem[0]} is about to be drained.

19.why: Preserve the value before popping so the animation label is correct.
19.invariant: splice_val about to be set; l1_rem has {len(l1_rem)} nodes remaining.

20.happening: About to drain {splice_val} from list1 into result.
20.why: List2 is gone; each remaining list1 node belongs at the end of the sorted result.
20.invariant: result will have {len(result)} + 1 nodes after this step.

21.happening: List1 is exhausted — list2 still has {len(l2_rem)} remaining nodes to drain.
21.why: Since list1 is empty, all remaining list2 nodes are already in sorted order relative to result.
21.invariant: result has {len(result)} nodes; l2_rem = {l2_rem} will be appended one-by-one.

21.happening: List2 is also empty — nothing left to drain from list2.
21.why: Both lists were fully consumed; the merge is complete.
21.invariant: result has {len(result)} nodes — the complete merged sequence.

22.happening: About to record splice_source = 'list2' for the drain animation.
22.why: Same animation as a regular splice — the node flies from list2 into result.
22.invariant: l2_rem[0] = {l2_rem[0]} is about to be drained.

23.why: Preserve the value before popping for the animation label.
23.invariant: splice_val about to be set; l2_rem has {len(l2_rem)} nodes remaining.

24.happening: About to drain {splice_val} from list2 into result.
24.why: List1 is gone; each remaining list2 node belongs at the end of the sorted result.
24.invariant: result will have {len(result)} + 1 nodes after this step.

25.happening: Returning result — the fully merged sorted list of {len(result)} nodes.
25.why: Both source lists are exhausted; result contains all nodes in non-decreasing order.
25.invariant: result = {result} — correct sorted merge of the two input lists.

init.happening: Setting up the working arrays for the three visual chains.
init.why: l1_rem and l2_rem will shrink as we consume nodes; result grows as we splice.
init.invariant: Input lists are unmodified; all working state is initialized.

loop.happening: Checking whether there are nodes remaining to compare or drain.
loop.why: The loop condition determines which phase of the merge we are in.

check.happening: Comparing the front values of list1 and list2.
check.why: The smaller front is always safe to append — it cannot be out-of-order with anything remaining.
check.invariant: The result chain is sorted up to its current length.

update.happening: Splicing the smaller node from its source list into result.
update.why: The core merge operation: the winning node leaves its chain and joins the result.
update.invariant: result grows by one node and remains sorted.

return.why: Both source lists are exhausted; result contains the complete sorted merge.
return.invariant: result has all input nodes in non-decreasing order.
